testcase.name: runScenario["Book appointment using Medicare", "Appointment Booking"](1)

@smoke Scenario: Book appointment using Medicare # src/test/resources/features/appointment.feature:11 Given User launches CURA website # steps.LoginSteps.launchWebsite() When User clicks Make Appointment # steps.LoginSteps.clickAppointment() And User enters valid username and password # steps.LoginSteps.enterCredentials() And User clicks Login button # steps.LoginSteps.clickLoginButton() And User selects Tokyo CURA Healthcare Center # steps.AppointmentSteps.selectFacility() org.openqa.selenium.NoSuchElementException: no such element: Unable to locate element: {"method":"css selector","selector":"#combo_facility"} (Session info: chrome=148.0.7778.181) For documentation on this error, please visit: And User selects Medicare program # steps.AppointmentSteps.selectMedicare() And User enters visit date # steps.AppointmentSteps.enterVisitDate() And User enters appointment comment # steps.AppointmentSteps.enterComment() And User books the appointment # steps.AppointmentSteps.bookAppointment() Then Appointment should be confirmed # steps.AppointmentSteps.verifyAppointment() Browser left open for debugging
testcase.system-err: SLF4J: Failed to load class "org.slf4j.impl.StaticLoggerBinder". SLF4J: Defaulting to no-operation (NOP) logger implementation SLF4J: See http://www.slf4j.org/codes.html#StaticLoggerBinder for further details. May 30, 2026 7:13:19 PM org.openqa.selenium.devtools.CdpVersionFinder findNearestMatch WARNING: Unable to find CDP implementation matching 148 May 30, 2026 7:13:19 PM org.openqa.selenium.chromium.ChromiumDriver lambda$new$5 WARNING: Unable to find version of CDP to use for 148.0.7778.181. You may need to include a dependency on a specific version of the CDP using something similar to `org.seleniumhq.selenium:selenium-devtools-v86:4.21.0` where the version ("v86") matches the version of the chromium-based browser you're using and the version number of the artifact is the same as Selenium's. testcase.name: runScenario["Successful Login", "Login Functionality"](2)
testcase.system-out: @smoke Scenario: Successful Login # src/test/resources/features/login.feature:4 Given User launches CURA website # steps.LoginSteps.launchWebsite() When User clicks Make Appointment # steps.LoginSteps.clickAppointment() And User enters valid username and password # steps.LoginSteps.enterCredentials() And User clicks Login button # steps.LoginSteps.clickLoginButton() Browser left open for debugging
